Bethell loses gold chain in RCB vs KKR match - here's what happened next
description: Jacob Bethell accidentally dropped his gold chain while getting dismissed during Royal Challengers Bengaluru’s IPL 2026 clash against Kolkata Knight Riders in Raipur. Devdutt Padikkal later picked it up and returned it to him. The match, however, belonged to Virat Kohli, whose unbeaten 105 guided RCB to a six-wicket win and the top of the points table.

title: 4 dead, 10 injured after bus-tanker collision triggers massive fire on Gujarat highway
description: A tragic collision between a private luxury bus and an asphalt-laden tanker near Sangani village in Gujarat's Surendranagar district resulted in a devastating fire. The incident, which occurred early Thursday morning, claimed the lives of at least four passengers and left ten others with burn injuries. The bus was en route from Ahmedabad to Rajkot when the accident happened.

title: ‘When you drop Virat Kohli, it becomes difficult’: Abhishek Nayar after KKR’s loss
'It’s going to finish one day': Virat Kohli makes honest reflection
description: Virat Kohli delivered a masterful performance, smashing his ninth IPL century to lead Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a successful chase of 193 against Kolkata Knight Riders. This historic innings saw Kohli become the fastest player to 14,000 T20 runs and the first Indian with 10 T20 centuries, solidifying his legacy as a supreme chaser.

description: A viral video from Hyderabad's Outer Ring Road shows a family traveling at over 100 kmph with at least three children seated in the car's open boot. The dangerous act has sparked outrage online, prompting Cyberabad Traffic Police to promise necessary action as per safety regulations.

Ahmedabad: Woman staffer caught on CCTV pocketing gold jewellery worth Rs 1.7 crore
description: A jeweller in Ahmedabad was shocked to discover an employee allegedly stole gold ornaments worth Rs 1.7 crore. CCTV footage reportedly shows the sales and marketing staffer pocketing chains, rings, and mangalsutras before leaving the showroom. Police are now actively searching for the accused woman.

title: BITS Goa reports 2nd student suicide this year; PhD scholar found dead
description: A 26-year-old PhD scholar from Kerala was found dead at a rented accommodation in Verna, marking the second student suicide at BITS Pilani's Goa Campus this year. Authorities are investigating the incident, with preliminary findings pointing to suicide. The recurring deaths are attributed to 'suicide contagion,' prompting the institution to enhance mental health support and reduce academic pressure.

Why rising temperatures are triggering more snakebite incidents across India
description: As summer heat intensifies, India faces a surge in deadly snakebite incidents, with states like Karnataka and Kerala reporting significant increases. Experts attribute this rise to snakes seeking cooler temperatures and escaping monsoon floods, leading to more human encounters. Delayed treatment and reliance on myths tragically cost lives, underscoring the urgent need for immediate medical attention and preventative measures.
